title: cmd/core/helpers.go env-reading helpers (parseFloatEnv, parseJSONArrayEnv, parseJSONObjectEnv) silently fall back to 0 / nil on missing or malformed env vars, codifying the FORBIDDEN fail-soft pattern under Gate G028 (NO-DEFAULTS / fail-loud SST policy)

scenarios.title: Dead-set helper symbols no longer exist anywhere in the repo
scenarios.given: the post-fix tree (after the design + plan + implement specialists have decided which helpers to delete vs convert vs keep)
scenarios.when: grep_search across all *.go files for the dead-set symbols (parseFloatEnv, parseJSONArrayEnv, parseJSONObjectEnv, parseJSONObject, parseJSONObjectVal — the 5 functions whose only callers were tests in cmd/core/main_test.go)
scenarios.then: the search returns ZERO matches anywhere in the repo (no source definitions in cmd/core/helpers.go, no test references in cmd/core/main_test.go, no comment mentions, no build-tagged stubs)
linkedTests: Verified post-fix by grep_search of cmd/core/helpers.go + cmd/core/main_test.go + cmd/core/connectors.go for the 5 dead-set symbols; refined by design specialist into a concrete in-tree audit script (e.g., a Go test that invokes go/parser to walk cmd/core/ AST for the symbol set, OR a build-time script under scripts/ invoked from ./smackerel.sh test unit --go)
scenarios.specSource: spec.md AC-2 (post-fix grep returns zero matches for the 5 dead-set symbols)
scenarios.redGreenProbe: Pre-fix: grep_search returns 24 test references + 5 source definitions = 29 hits. Post-fix: grep_search returns zero hits. Reverting any of the deletions causes the symbol-removal audit to FAIL with a non-zero hit count.
scenarios.adversarial: YES — re-introducing any of the 5 dead-set symbols (e.g., a future maintainer adding `func parseFloatEnv(key string) float64 { ... }` back to helpers.go to support a new connector) MUST cause this scenario to FAIL with the explicit symbol-name + line number of the offending re-introduction.

scenarios.title: cmd/core/helpers.go contains no os.Getenv silent-fallback patterns
scenarios.when: grep_search of cmd/core/helpers.go for any function whose body contains os.Getenv(key) followed by a return-on-empty path that does not propagate an error
scenarios.then: the search returns ZERO matches; any env reads remaining in cmd/core/helpers.go (e.g., from helpers transitively kept alive by live string-form callers) MUST follow the Gate G028 canonical Go pattern (os.Getenv(KEY) + empty/parse-error → log.Fatal OR (value, error) propagation)
linkedTests: Refined by design specialist into a concrete in-tree audit (Go test that walks cmd/core/helpers.go AST for the silent-fallback pattern signature)
scenarios.specSource: spec.md AC-3 (no silent-fallback patterns in cmd/core/helpers.go) + Gate G028 / .github/instructions/smackerel-no-defaults.instructions.md REQUIRED Go column
scenarios.redGreenProbe: Pre-fix: cmd/core/helpers.go contains 3 functions matching the silent-fallback shape (parseFloatEnv lines 65-83, parseJSONArrayEnv lines 19-22, parseJSONObjectEnv lines 45-48). Post-fix: zero matches. Re-introducing the silent-fallback pattern in any helper causes the audit to FAIL.
scenarios.adversarial: YES — adding ANY new function to cmd/core/helpers.go that matches `os.Getenv(KEY)` followed by `return <literal> | return nil` without propagating an error MUST cause the pattern audit to FAIL. The audit catches both the original 3 dead-set helpers AND any future copy-paste re-introduction.

scenarios.title: Persistent in-tree adversarial regression guard catches future re-introduction of the FORBIDDEN pattern
scenarios.given: the post-fix tree includes a persistent regression guard (Go test, build-tag check, or go vet analyzer) committed in cmd/core/ or scripts/
scenarios.when: the regression guard runs as part of every ./smackerel.sh test unit --go invocation
scenarios.then: the guard PASSES against the post-fix tree, AND fails RED if a future maintainer re-introduces ANY of the dead-set symbols OR any new function matching the silent-fallback signature shape; the failure message names the offending file + line + symbol
linkedTests: Refined by design + plan specialists into a concrete test name + file path (e.g., cmd/core/helpers_no_defaults_test.go::TestNoSilentFallbackHelpers)
scenarios.specSource: spec.md AC-5 (adversarial regression guard mechanically catches future re-introduction)
scenarios.redGreenProbe: Pre-fix (with the dead-set helpers still in place + the new guard added): the guard FAILS, reporting the 3 silent-fallback helpers by name + line. Post-fix (helpers deleted): the guard PASSES. Re-introducing any of the deleted helpers causes the guard to FAIL again with the same message shape — proving the guard mechanically catches the regression.
scenarios.adversarial: YES — this scenario IS the persistent regression guard. Its failure mode is the canonical signal that a Gate G028 violation re-entered the codebase via cmd/core/helpers.go.

scenarios.title: Existing Go unit test suite passes unchanged after the deletion
scenarios.given: the post-fix tree with the 5 dead-set helpers and their tests deleted
scenarios.when: ./smackerel.sh test unit --go runs against the post-fix tree
scenarios.then: every Go test in the cmd/core package PASSes; the test count drops by 24 (the 16 + 5 + 3 = 24 test functions that exercised parseFloatEnv / parseJSONArrayEnv / parseJSONObjectEnv) plus 7 (parseJSONObject tests) = 31 deleted test functions; ZERO pre-existing tests for unrelated cmd/core code regress
linkedTests: All pre-existing cmd/core/main_test.go tests for non-deleted helpers (parseJSONArray test cases at lines 109, 119, 126, 136, 143, 150, 158)
All pre-existing cmd/core test files (TestAllConnectorsRegistered etc.)
Repo-wide Go suite under ./smackerel.sh test unit --go
scenarios.specSource: spec.md AC-6 (existing Go unit tests pass after deletion; live callers untouched)
scenarios.redGreenProbe: Pre-fix: ./smackerel.sh test unit --go reports N tests PASS. Post-fix: ./smackerel.sh test unit --go reports (N - 31) tests PASS. Any test count delta beyond the planned -31 indicates an unintended regression.
scenarios.adversarial: NO — positive canary that the broader Go test suite is unaffected by the deletion. Adversarial coverage lives in SCN-020-003-A through SCN-020-003-C.

scenarios.title: Live production callers of parseJSONArray remain untouched and continue to behave identically
scenarios.given: the post-fix tree (the 5 dead-set helpers deleted, but parseJSONArray + parseJSONArrayVal preserved because they have live production callers)
scenarios.then: lines 76 (`parseJSONArray(cfg.BookmarksExcludeDomains)`) and 103 (`parseJSONArray(cfg.BrowserHistoryCustomSkipDomains)`) compile unchanged; the bookmarks + browser-history connector wiring blocks produce identical params maps as the pre-fix tree (verified by grep_search confirming no diff in those call sites + by passing connector wiring tests)
scenarios.specSource: spec.md Out of Scope (parseJSONArray + parseJSONArrayVal are LIVE; not touched in this packet)
scenarios.redGreenProbe: Pre-fix and post-fix: grep_search of cmd/core/connectors.go for parseJSONArray returns identical line numbers + identical surrounding context. The 7 parseJSONArray test cases in cmd/core/main_test.go continue to PASS unchanged.
scenarios.adversarial: NO — positive canary that the bounded-deletion contract is honoured. Adversarial coverage for the live-symbol preservation lives in the broader Go build (compile failure is the failure mode if parseJSONArray is accidentally deleted).
